name: Lex Fridman Podcast (AI episodes)
notes_head: A long-form interview podcast hosted by Lex Fridman featuring in-depth conversations with AI researchers, policymakers, and technologists about artificial intelligence and its societal implications.
key_arg: Long-form interviews with AI leaders including Altman, Zuckerberg, Musk, Hassabis on capabilities and safety.

name: Crisp and Fuzzy Tasks
notes_head: Essay distinguishing between ''crisp'' tasks (well-defined, easily measurable) and ''fuzzy'' tasks (ambiguous, judgment-dependent) in the context of AI capabilities and automation.
key_arg: Distinguishes between tasks AI excels at (crisp, well-defined) vs tasks where human judgment remains essential (fuzzy, context-dependent).

name: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
resource_author: Jared Kaplan, Sam McCandlish, Tom Henighan, Tom B. Brown, Benjamin Chess, Rewon Child, Scott Gray, Alec Radford, Jeffrey Wu, Dario Amodei (OpenAI)
notes_head: 2020 OpenAI paper by Jared Kaplan, Sam McCandlish, and team (many of whom later co-founded Anthropic) that established the empirical scaling-laws framework. A foundational reference for policy debates about compute governance, export controls, and the inevitability/contingency of capability gains. Widely cited alongside the Chinchilla paper (Hoffmann et al. 2022).
key_arg: Empirically demonstrates that language model performance follows smooth power-law relationships across model size, dataset size, and training compute. Motivated the large-scale investment in ever-larger models (GPT-3 and successors) by showing predictable capability gains from compute scaling.

name: The AI Index Report 2024
notes_head: TEST DATA — Most-cited annual report on AI ecosystem trends
key_arg: Comprehensive annual benchmarking of AI progress, investment, policy, and public opinion

name: Why AI Will Save the World
notes_head: Essay by Marc Andreessen (June 2023) arguing that AI will be a profoundly positive force for humanity, countering doomer narratives with a case for accelerating AI development.
key_arg: AI risk is overstated by incumbents seeking regulatory moats; AI will generate enormous prosperity

name: Governing AI: A Blueprint for the Future
notes_head: TEST DATA — Influential policy blueprint cited by Senate AI Working Group
key_arg: Proposes tiered governance framework based on AI capability levels

name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
notes_head: TEST DATA — De facto standard for AI risk management in US industry
key_arg: Voluntary framework for managing AI risks throughout the AI lifecycle

name: SB 53 (CA)
notes_head: California Senate Bill 53, an AI safety bill requiring developers of frontier AI models to implement shutdown capabilities and safety protocols for powerful AI systems.

name: RAISE Act
notes_head: The New York Responsible AI Safety and Education (RAISE) Act, signed by Governor Hochul in December 2025, requiring frontier AI developers to publish and comply with safety and security governance plans.

name: A Guide to the AI Tribes
notes_head: Essay by Michel Justen (January 2026) mapping the major ideological factions in AI policy debate — their origins, assumptions, alliances, and conflicts over how AI should be developed and governed.

name: The Left is Missing Out on AI
notes_head: Essay by Dan Kagan-Kans (February 2026) arguing that the political left has ceded the AI conversation to the right by dismissing AI as overhyped, risking inability to shape one of the most consequential technological shifts in history.

name: AI 2027 scenario
notes_head: Scenario and forecast paper by Daniel Kokotajlo et al. projecting a plausible timeline for transformative AI development through 2027, exploring rapid capability gains and their implications for safety and governance.

name: AI as Normal Technology
notes_head: Essay by Arvind Narayanan and Sayash Kapoor arguing that AI should be understood as a transformative but ''normal'' technology — like electricity or the internet — rather than as an unprecedented existential force requiring extraordinary governance.

name: Dignity in a Digital Age
notes_head: Book by Rep. Ro Khanna (2022) advocating for policies that bring technology jobs and digital infrastructure to overlooked communities across America, bridging the gap between Silicon Valley and the rest of the country.

name: The Entrepreneurial State
notes_head: Book by Mariana Mazzucato (2013) challenging the myth that innovation comes only from the private sector, arguing that government investment has been the driving force behind transformative technologies including the internet and smartphones.

name: Power and Progress
notes_head: Book by Daron Acemoglu and Simon Johnson (2023) examining how technological progress has historically benefited elites unless countered by deliberate institutional choices, and arguing for redirecting AI toward broadly shared prosperity.

name: Recoding America
notes_head: Book by Jennifer Pahlka (2023) diagnosing why government technology systems fail and outlining how modernizing digital infrastructure is essential for effective public services and democratic accountability.

name: Governing the Commons
notes_head: Seminal book by Elinor Ostrom (1990) demonstrating that communities can successfully manage shared resources through self-governance without requiring privatization or top-down state control.

name: Techno-Optimist Manifesto
notes_head: Marc Andreessen''s manifesto (October 2023) published on the a16z website, making an expansive case for technological optimism and arguing against precautionary regulation of emerging technologies including AI.

name: The Internet Con
notes_head: Book by Cory Doctorow (2023) arguing that Big Tech companies maintain dominance through lock-in and switching costs, and proposing interoperability mandates as the key to breaking their grip on users and markets.

name: Chip War
notes_head: Book by Chris Miller (2022) tracing the geopolitical history of the global semiconductor industry and explaining how control over chip manufacturing has become a critical factor in international power dynamics and AI competition.

name: Secure AI Project bills tracker
notes_head: Online tracking tool maintained by the Secure AI Project that monitors and catalogs AI-related legislation at the state and federal level, providing real-time status updates on bills and regulatory proposals.

name: Dean Ball "How I Approach AI Policy"
notes_head: Blog post by Dean Ball outlining his analytical framework for evaluating AI policy proposals, emphasizing pragmatic governance approaches that balance innovation with risk mitigation.

name: Digitalist Papers Vol. 2
notes_head: Volume 2 of The Digitalist Papers, published by the Stanford Digital Economy Lab (December 2025), a collection of 21 essays examining the economic implications of transformative AI, including proposals for universal basic capital and adaptive safety nets.

name: Alex Bores Congressional AI Framework
notes_head: Congressional AI policy framework released by New York Assemblyman Alex Bores (February 2026) as part of his campaign for Congress, proposing an eight-point national plan covering kids'' safety, data privacy, deepfakes, workforce impacts, and frontier AI safety testing.

name: Trump AI Action Plan (Jan 2025)
notes_head: Executive order signed by President Trump in January 2025 establishing the administration''s AI policy direction, emphasizing deregulation and American competitiveness in AI development.

name: Sacks federal preemption EO (Dec 2025)
notes_head: Executive order orchestrated by AI czar David Sacks (December 2025) asserting federal preemption over state-level AI regulations, aiming to create a unified national approach and prevent a patchwork of state AI laws.

name: EU AI Act
notes_head: The European Union''s comprehensive AI regulation framework, entered into force in August 2024, establishing a risk-based classification system for AI applications with tiered requirements ranging from transparency obligations to outright bans on certain uses.

name: New Consensus AI platform
notes_head: New Consensus is a progressive economic think tank whose AI platform advocates for public investment and democratic governance of artificial intelligence.

name: Secure AI Project state bill tracker
notes_head: A tracking tool maintained by the Secure AI Project that monitors and catalogs state-level AI legislation across the United States.

name: Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights
notes_head: Published by the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy in October 2022, this non-binding framework outlines five principles to protect the public from harms caused by automated systems.

name: SB 1047 (CA, vetoed)
notes_head: California Senate Bill 1047, authored by Senator Scott Wiener, would have imposed safety requirements on developers of frontier AI models; it was vetoed by Governor Newsom in September 2024.

name: Colorado AI anti-discrimination law
notes_head: Colorado SB 24-205, signed into law in 2024, is the first U.S. state law specifically regulating algorithmic discrimination in high-risk AI decision-making systems.

name: Platformer
notes_head: A technology newsletter by journalist Casey Newton, published on Substack, covering the intersection of social media platforms, technology policy, and the tech industry.
key_arg: Platformer covers technology policy and corporate accountability, including AI-related developments in content moderation and platform governance.

name: AI Snake Oil
notes_head: A book by Princeton researchers Arvind Narayanan and Sayash Kapoor critically examining exaggerated claims about AI capabilities and the harms caused by deploying unreliable AI systems.
key_arg: Critical perspective on AI hype, distinguishing genuine capabilities from marketing claims.

name: Executive Order on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI
notes_head: The formal title of President Biden''s October 2023 Executive Order (EO 14110) on artificial intelligence, establishing federal standards for AI safety, security, and trustworthiness.
key_arg: Biden EO establishing safety standards, requiring reporting of large training runs, and directing agency action on AI.

name: The Adolescence of Technology
notes_head: Dario Amodei''s essay conceptualizing the current era of AI development as a ''technological adolescence'' — a volatile transitional period requiring deliberate governance and safety measures.
key_arg: AI development is in a critical adolescent phase where we must choose between responsible maturation and reckless acceleration.

name: Trump Attempts to Preempt State AI Regulation Through Executive Order
notes_head: A news article reporting on President Trump''s executive order attempting to preempt state-level AI regulations in favor of a unified federal approach to AI governance.
key_arg: President Trump's executive order preempts state AI regulations by establishing federal agency actions and framework for future legislation.

name: Trump signs executive order blocking states from enforcing their own regulations around AI | CNN Business
notes_head: A CNN Business article covering President Trump''s executive order aimed at blocking states from enforcing their own AI regulations, centralizing AI policy at the federal level.
key_arg: President Trump signed an executive order establishing federal preemption of state AI regulations to create a unified national framework for AI governance.

name: Open Problems in Technical AI Governance
resource_author: Centre for the Governance of AI
notes_head: A research paper identifying and analyzing unresolved technical challenges in AI governance, including issues of compliance verification, auditing, and enforcement of AI regulations.
key_arg: Identifies key unsolved technical problems at the intersection of AI capabilities and governance, providing a research agenda for the field.

name: A Collection of AI Governance Research Ideas
notes_head: Research paper compiling a collection of open research questions and ideas in the field of AI governance, intended to guide future scholarship and policy work.
key_arg: Curated database of research questions and project ideas for advancing AI governance.

name: The Federal AI Preemption Push: President Trump signs the Executive Order titled “Ensuring a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence”
notes_head: Legal analysis article examining President Trump''s executive order on federal AI preemption, which aims to establish a unified national AI policy framework by overriding state-level AI regulations.
key_arg: Trump's executive order on a national AI policy framework asserts federal dominance over AI regulation by preempting state laws deemed inconsistent with national policy objectives.

name: Common Ground between AI 2027 & AI as Normal Technology
notes_head: Essay exploring the points of agreement between the AI 2027 scenario (which envisions rapid, transformative AI progress) and the ''AI as Normal Technology'' perspective (which views AI as an incremental, manageable advance).
key_arg: Identifies shared assumptions between the accelerationist and cautious AI narratives, arguing the two camps agree on more than they think.

name: Computing Power and the Governance of Artificial Intelligence
notes_head: Research paper analyzing the role of computing power as a governance lever for artificial intelligence, exploring how compute resources can be monitored, allocated, and regulated to shape AI development outcomes.
key_arg: Compute is a key lever for AI governance because it is detectable, excludable, and quantifiable, making it uniquely suitable for regulation.

name: Transcript: US Senate Subcommittee Hearing on "Protecting Consumers from Artificial Intelligence Enabled Fraud and Scams" | TechPolicy.Press
notes_head: Transcript of a U.S. Senate subcommittee hearing focused on protecting consumers from AI-enabled fraud and scams, published by TechPolicy.Press.
key_arg: Congressional testimony addressing consumer protection from AI-enabled fraud through increased platform accountability, content authentication, and consumer education.

name: White House Issues "One Rule" Executive Order To Curb State AI Regulation - New Technology - United States
notes_head: Legal analysis article (likely from Mondaq) examining the White House''s ''one rule'' executive order aimed at curbing state-level AI regulation through federal preemption. Covers similar ground to entities 661 and 681.
key_arg: A White House executive order establishing a national AI regulation standard to preempt conflicting state laws and create uniform federal policy.

name: Some Talent Needs in AI Governance
notes_head: Essay identifying key talent gaps and workforce needs in the AI governance field, discussing what skills and roles are most urgently needed to support effective AI policy and oversight.
key_arg: Identifies critical talent gaps in AI governance including technical policy expertise, international coordination, and compute governance.

name: Toward A National AI Framework: The Federal Strategy To Override State Regulation - New Technology - United States
notes_head: Legal analysis article examining the federal strategy to establish a national AI framework that would override state-level regulation, covering the same Trump AI preemption executive order discussed in entities 661 and 674.
key_arg: The Trump Administration's Executive Order seeks to establish a federal AI framework that prioritizes national competitiveness while preempting state-level AI regulations.

name: The Annual AI Governance Report 2025
notes_head: Comprehensive annual report surveying the AI governance landscape in 2025, covering policy developments, institutional activities, and emerging trends in AI regulation and oversight worldwide.
key_arg: Coordinated international efforts, standards, and ethical frameworks are essential for managing AI's global impact and ensuring responsible governance.

name: The Annual AI Governance Report 2025: Steering the Future of AI
notes_head: Annual report examining the state of AI governance in 2025, covering policy frameworks, regulatory developments, and strategies for steering AI''s future trajectory.
key_arg: Effective AI governance requires global, multi-stakeholder collaboration to develop inclusive and ethical frameworks that maximize AI's benefits while mitigating risks.

name: AI Governance Profession Report 2025
notes_head: Report surveying the AI governance profession landscape in 2025, including roles, skills, and career trends for practitioners working in AI policy and oversight.
key_arg: Organizations are increasingly establishing AI governance programs to ensure responsible and compliant AI deployment across their operations.

name: Transcript: US House Subcommittee Hosts Hearing on "AI ...
notes_head: Transcript of a U.S. House subcommittee hearing examining artificial intelligence policy, published by TechPolicy.Press.
key_arg: Congressional debate on AI regulation reflected partisan divisions, with Democrats prioritizing consumer protections while Republicans opposed proposed moratoriums on AI development.

name: Adam Thierer Testimony before JEC on AI & Economic Opportunity
notes_head: Written testimony by Adam Thierer before the Joint Economic Committee on the economic opportunities presented by AI and the policy conditions needed to realize them.
key_arg: AI presents substantial opportunities for economic growth and productivity gains across sectors, though realizing these benefits requires appropriate policy frameworks that avoid over-regulation.

name: The Federal AI Preemption Push: President Trump Signs The Executive Order Titled "Ensuring A National Policy Framework For Artificial Intelligence" - New Technology - United States
notes_head: Legal analysis published on Mondaq examining President Trump''s executive order on ensuring a national AI policy framework, focusing on its implications for federal preemption of state AI regulations.
key_arg: President Trump signed an executive order establishing a national AI policy framework designed to preempt state-level AI regulations.

name: Blackburn Unveils National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence - U.S. S...
notes_head: Announcement by Senator Marsha Blackburn of a national policy framework for artificial intelligence, outlining proposed federal AI governance principles.
key_arg: Senator Blackburn's legislative proposal for the TRUMP AMERICA AI Act, establishing a unified federal framework for AI policy with protections for children and consumers.

name: Situational Awareness
notes_head: TEST DATA — Viral essay by former OpenAI researcher; influential in policy circles
key_arg: AGI is imminent; the US must win the race against China or face catastrophic consequences

name: Statement on AI Risk
notes_head: TEST DATA — One-sentence statement signed by hundreds of AI researchers and public figures
key_arg: Mitigating the risk of extinction from AI should be a global priority alongside pandemics and nuclear war

name: The Alignment Problem
notes_head: TEST DATA — Accessible introduction to alignment for non-technical readers
key_arg: Comprehensive overview of the AI alignment challenge, from fairness to existential risk

name: AI Frontiers
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Platform from the Center for AI Safety (CAIS) posting articles written by experts from a wide range of fields discussing the impacts of AI.

name: AI Policy Bulletin
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Publishes policy-relevant perspectives on frontier AI governance, including research summaries, opinions, interviews, and explainers.

name: AI Prospects
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog by Eric Drexler on AI prospects and their surprising implications for technology, economics, environmental concerns, and military affairs.

name: Astral Codex Ten
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog covering many topics, including reasoning, science, psychiatry, medicine, ethics, genetics, economics, politics, and AI. Often book summaries and commentary on AI safety.

name: Bounded Regret
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog on AI safety by Jacob Steinhardt, a UC Berkeley statistics professor, analysing risks, forecasting future breakthroughs, and discussing alignment strategies.

name: ChinaTalk
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Deep, thoughtful coverage on China, technology, and US-China relations – written by Jordan Schneider.

name: Cold Takes
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog about transformative AI, futurism, research, ethics, philanthropy etc. by Holden Karnofsky. Includes the "Most Important Century" post series. Last posted in 2024.

name: Daniel Paleka's Newsletter
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog by AI safety researcher Daniel Paleka curating and succinctly analyzing the latest research and news in AI safety. Posts about every two months.

name: DeepMind Safety Research
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog from the Google DeepMind safety team discussing research ideas about building AI safely.

name: Don't Worry about the Vase
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog by Zvi Mowshowitz on various topics, including AI, offering detailed analysis, personal insights, and a rationalist perspective. Posts very frequently.

name: Miles's Substack
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog from ex-OpenAI (now independent) AI policy researcher Miles Brundage on the rapid evolution of AI and the urgent need for thoughtful governance.

name: Obsolete
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Reporting and analysis on capitalism, great power competition, and the race to build superintelligence by freelance journalist Garrison Lovely.

name: Paul Christiano's Blog
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog on aligning prosaic AI by one of the leading AI safety researchers. No longer active but the archive is high quality.

name: Planned Obsolescence
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Substack by Ajeya Cotra, researcher at METR, covering AI capabilities forecasting, AI agent benchmarks, timelines for automating AI R&D, and implications for AI safety.

name: Rising Tide
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog by Helen Toner (director of CSET and former OpenAI board member) offering analysis on navigating the transition to advanced AI systems.

name: The Building Capacity Blog
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog about building the fields of AI safety and Effective Altruism, discussing big-picture strategy and sharing personal experience from working in the space.

name: The Power Law
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Top forecaster Peter Wildeford forecasts the future and discusses AI, national security, innovation, emerging technology, and the powers – real and metaphorical – that shape the world.

name: Threading the Needle
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Publication by Anton Leicht on the political economy of AI progress, featuring weekly posts on how institutions and political incentives interface with fast technological change.

name: Victoria Krakovna's Blog
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Blog by an AI safety researcher at Google DeepMind, covering alignment research, rationality, and personal productivity insights. Last AI post was 2023.

name: AISafety.com: Media channels
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. The AI safety space is changing rapidly. This directory of key information sources can help you keep up to date with the latest developments.

name: AI Safety at the Frontier
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Johannes Gasteiger, an alignment researcher at Anthropic, selects and summarizes the most interesting AI safety papers each month.

name: AI Safety Events & Training
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Weekly newsletter listing newly-announced AI safety events and training programs, both online and in-person.

name: AI Safety Funding
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Newsletter listing newly announced funding opportunities for individuals and organizations working to reduce AI existential risk.

name: AI Safety in China
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Newsletter from Concordia AI, a Beijing-based social enterprise, providing updates on AI safety developments in China.

name: AI Safety Newsletter
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Newsletter published every few weeks discussing recent developments in AI and AI safety. No technical background required.

name: Can We Secure AI With Formal Methods?
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Current-events newsletter for keeping up to date with FMxAI (formal methods and AI) with a gear toward safety, doing a mix of shallow technical reviews of papers and movement updates.

name: Import AI
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Weekly updates on the latest developments in AI research (including governance) written by Jack Clark, co-founder of Anthropic.

name: ML Safety Newsletter
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Infrequent newsletter aiming to inform readers about recent ML safety research, focussing on areas like adversarial robustness, interpretability, and control.

name: Transformer
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Aiming to help decision-makers understand what’s happening in AI and why it matters – through news roundups, explainers, features, and opinion pieces.

name: 80,000 Hours Podcast
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. In-depth conversations about the world’s most pressing problems – including AI safety – and what you can do to help solve them.

name: AI X-risk Research Podcast
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Interviews with (mostly technical) AI safety researchers about their research, aiming to get a sense of why it was written and how it might reduce existential risk from AI.

name: Dwarkesh Podcast
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Well-researched interviews with influential intellectuals going in-depth on AI and technology, and their broader societal implications.

name: Future of Life Institute Podcast
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Interviews with existential risk researchers, policy experts, philosophers, and a range of other influential thinkers.

name: The AI Policy Podcast
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Podcast from the Center for Strategic & International Studies (CSIS) discussing AI regulation, innovation, national security, and geopolitics.

name: The Cognitive Revolution
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Biweekly podcast where host Nathan Labenz interviews AI innovators and thinkers, discussing the transformative impact AI will likely have in the near future.

name: AI Alignment Slack
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. The biggest real-time online community of people interested in AI safety, with channels ranging from general topics to specific fields to local groups.

name: AI Digest
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Concise visual explainers of important trends in AI, grounded in concrete examples of what AI models can do right now.

name: AI Risk Explorer
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Online platform monitoring the emergence of large-scale AI risks, featuring curated information on evaluations, incidents, and policies.

name: AI Risk: Why Care?
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. AI safety chatbot that excels at addressing hard questions and counterarguments about existential risk.

name: AI Safety for Fleshy Humans
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Accessible, comic-illustrated series describing the history of AI capabilities, the alignment problem, and potential solutions.

name: AI Safety Map Anki Deck
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Flashcards for helping to learn and memorize the main organizations, projects, and programs currently operating in the AI safety space.

name: AI Safety Support
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Field-building organization now chiefly serving as the home for an extensive resources list called Lots of Links.

name: AI Watch
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Database tracking people, organizations, and “products” in the AI safety community, serving as a reference for positions, affiliations, and related data.

name: AISafety.com
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Hub for key resources for the AI safety community, including directories of courses, jobs, upcoming events and training programs etc. – and this map!

name: Effective Altruism Domains
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Directory of domains freely available to be used for high-impact projects, including those contributing to AI safety.

name: Effective Altruism Forum
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Forum on doing good as effectively as possible, including AI safety. Also has a podcast featuring text-to-speech narrations of top posts.

name: LessWrong
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Online forum dedicated to improving human reasoning, containing a lot of AI safety content. Also has a podcast featuring text-to-speech narrations of top posts.

name: MIT AI Risk Repository
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Comprehensive living database of over 1600 AI risks, categorized by their cause and risk domain.

name: RiesgosIA.org
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Spanish-language website featuring AI safety educational tools, including a daily‑updated monitor of AI safety research papers.

name: AI Explained
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. YouTuber discussing the latest AI developments as they happen, offering explanations and analysis of important research and events.

name: AI In Context
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Channel produced by 80,000 Hours presenting thoroughly researched, cinematic stories about what's happening in AI and where the trends are taking us.

name: AI Safety Videos
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Comprehensive directory of AI safety video content, from beginner-friendly introductions to in-depth expert talks.

name: Doom Debates
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Channel hosted by Liron Shapira featuring in-depth debates, explainers, and live Q&A sessions, focused on AI existential risk and other implications of superintelligence.

name: Dr Waku
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Computer science PhD and AI research scientist talking about how AI will likely affect the world, along with expert interviews.

name: FAR․AI YouTube channel
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Talks given by AI safety experts at various events, covering topics ranging from mechanistic interpretability to evals and governance.

name: Lethal Intelligence
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. YouTube channel raising awareness about the lethal dangers of AGI through explainer videos and podcasts.

name: Rational Animations
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Animated videos aiming to foster good thinking, promote altruistic causes, and help ensure humanity's future goes well – particularly regarding AI safety.

name: Robert Miles
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. The most popular AI safety education channel, explaining technical alignment concepts to general audiences through accessible explainer videos.

name: Siliconversations
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. YouTube channel explaining (mostly) AI safety concepts through entertaining stickman videos.

name: Species
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. Channel run by Drew Spartz educating a general audience about AI risk through high-effort mini-documentaries.

name: The AI Risk Network
notes_head: From AISafety.com map. YouTube channel run by John Sherman, a Peabody and Emmy Award-winning former investigative journalist, aiming to make AI risk a kitchen table conversation.

name: Human Compatible
notes_head: TEST DATA — Foundational text on AI alignment for general audience
key_arg: Current AI paradigm of fixed objectives is fundamentally dangerous; need provably beneficial AI

name: The Precipice: Existential Risk and the Future of Humanity
notes_head: A 2020 book by philosopher Toby Ord arguing that safeguarding humanity''s long-term future from existential risks, including advanced AI, is among the most important moral priorities of our time.
key_arg: Argues this century is a critical period for existential risk, with unaligned AI as the greatest threat.

name: The Urgency of Interpretability
notes_head: A paper or talk making the case that understanding the internal workings of AI models (interpretability) is an urgent priority for ensuring safe and trustworthy AI systems.
key_arg: Understanding how AI models work internally is critical for safety and must be prioritized alongside capability development.

name: On the Responsible Scaling of AI
notes_head: Anthropic''s policy paper outlining its Responsible Scaling Policy, a framework for incrementally deploying more capable AI models while managing catastrophic risks through safety evaluations.
key_arg: Framework for when and how to scale AI safely, with commitments to pause if safety evaluations show dangerous capabilities.

name: Superintelligence: Paths, Dangers, Strategies
notes_head: A 2014 book by Oxford philosopher Nick Bostrom analyzing the potential trajectories, dangers, and strategic challenges posed by the development of machine superintelligence.
key_arg: Foundational text arguing that superintelligent AI could pose existential risk if not aligned with human values.

name: International AI Safety Report
notes_head: A 2024 international report led by Yoshua Bengio and a global panel of experts assessing the current state of AI safety risks and recommending governance approaches.
key_arg: Expert consensus report on AI risks and safety measures, commissioned by the UK AI Safety Summit.

name: Zvi Mowshowitz AI Newsletter
notes_head: A weekly AI analysis newsletter by Zvi Mowshowitz (also known as ''Don''t Worry About the Vase'') providing detailed commentary on AI developments, policy, and safety research.
key_arg: Comprehensive weekly analysis of AI developments with focus on safety and existential risk.

name: Life 3.0: Being Human in the Age of AI
notes_head: A 2017 book by MIT physicist Max Tegmark exploring how artificial intelligence will transform life on Earth and examining the future trajectory of intelligent life in the universe.
key_arg: Humanity must proactively engage in AI safety research and develop wise governance strategies to ensure advanced AI remains beneficial and aligned with human values.

name: Highlights of the 2023 Executive Order on Artificial ...
notes_head: Summary article highlighting the key provisions of President Biden''s 2023 executive order on artificial intelligence, which established new safety standards, equity protections, and innovation support measures for AI development.
key_arg: This Congressional Research Service report summarizes the 2023 Executive Order establishing a comprehensive government-wide approach to ensure safe, secure, and trustworthy AI development and deployment.

name: Foundational Challenges in Assuring Alignment and Safety of Large Language Models
notes_head: Academic paper examining the foundational technical and conceptual challenges in ensuring that large language models are aligned with human values and operate safely.
key_arg: Comprehensive survey of fundamental open problems in LLM alignment, including specification, assurance, and societal impacts.

name: Alignment Remains a Hard, Unsolved Problem
notes_head: Essay or paper arguing that AI alignment — ensuring AI systems reliably act in accordance with human intentions and values — remains a fundamentally difficult and unsolved problem despite recent progress.
key_arg: Argues that despite recent progress, the fundamental problem of ensuring AI systems reliably pursue human values remains unsolved.

name: US Senate Hearing On 'Examining the Harm of AI Chatbots'
notes_head: Official U.S. Senate hearing titled ''Examining the Harm of AI Chatbots,'' investigating the potential risks and harms posed by AI chatbot technologies to consumers and society.
key_arg: AI chatbots pose significant safety risks, particularly to children, as demonstrated by documented cases of self-harm and exploitation, necessitating congressional regulatory action.

name: Alignment Pretraining: AI Discourse Causes Self-Fulfilling (Mis)alignment
notes_head: Research paper investigating how public discourse about AI alignment and safety can become self-fulfilling — shaping AI behavior during pretraining in ways that either help or hinder actual alignment outcomes.
key_arg: How AI models trained on alignment discourse may develop self-fulfilling beliefs about their own alignment or misalignment.

name: Values in the Wild
notes_head: Research paper studying human values as they manifest ''in the wild'' — examining how values are embedded in, expressed through, and shaped by real-world AI systems and their deployment contexts.
key_arg: Empirical study of how Claude's values manifest in real-world usage, examining the gap between intended behavior and actual deployment.

name: The Persona Selection Model: Why AI Assistants Might Behave Like Humans
resource_author: Samuel Marks, Alex Lindsey, Chris Olah
notes_head: Research paper proposing a ''persona selection model'' to explain why AI assistants may adopt human-like behavioral patterns, exploring the mechanisms by which LLMs select and enact particular personas.
key_arg: Proposes that LLMs select from a repertoire of learned personas rather than having a single fixed character, with implications for alignment.

name: The Assistant Axis: Situating and Stabilizing the Character of LLMs
notes_head: Research paper on methods for situating and stabilizing the character or persona of large language models along an ''assistant axis,'' addressing consistency and reliability in LLM behavior.
key_arg: Framework for understanding and stabilizing the character dimensions of language model assistants.

name: Gradual Disempowerment
resource_author: MISSING - actual author unknown from provided data
notes_head: Research paper examining how AI systems could gradually erode human control and autonomy over time, even without a single catastrophic event.
key_arg: AI systems may gradually erode human control and decision-making authority through incremental shifts in power, raising concerns about long-term human agency.

name: AI Safety Index 2025: How Frontier AI Companies | Libertify
notes_head: Coverage by Libertify of the Future of Life Institute''s 2025 AI Safety Index, which benchmarks how frontier AI companies manage safety practices.
key_arg: The AI Safety Index 2025 benchmarks frontier AI companies on safety practices and risk management, showing significant variation in governance maturity.

name: 2025 AI Safety Index - Future of Life Institute
notes_head: The Future of Life Institute''s 2025 AI Safety Index, a benchmarking report assessing how leading AI companies address safety in their development practices.
key_arg: While leading AI companies like Anthropic and OpenAI are advancing safety assessments and transparency measures, the broader AI industry lacks comprehensive safety standards and preparedness frameworks.

name: AI Safety Index Winter 2025 - Future of Life Institute
notes_head: The Winter 2025 edition of the Future of Life Institute''s AI Safety Index, evaluating frontier AI companies'' safety commitments and practices.
key_arg: The Winter 2025 AI Safety Index reveals substantial variations in safety practices among leading AI companies, with Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google demonstrating stronger safety governance than industry peers.

name: Senate Judiciary written testimony - Helen Toner 2024-09-17
notes_head: Written testimony by Helen Toner, former OpenAI board member, before the Senate Judiciary Committee in September 2024, offering an insider''s perspective on AI governance and safety.
key_arg: AI companies developing toward artificial general intelligence view AGI as a serious and imminent goal, raising urgent policy questions about oversight and safety measures needed before advanced AI systems are deployed.

name: Transcript: Senate Judiciary Subcommittee Hosts Hearing on Oversight of AI: Insiders’ Perspectives | TechPolicy.Press
notes_head: Transcript of a Senate Judiciary Subcommittee hearing featuring insider perspectives on AI oversight, published by TechPolicy.Press.
key_arg: Industry insiders testify before the Senate Judiciary Subcommittee on the necessity for enforceable AI regulations and corporate accountability measures to balance innovation with safety and public interest.

name: 2025 Q4 Update from our Frontier AI Risk Monitoring Platform
notes_head: Quarterly update (Q4 2025) from a frontier AI risk monitoring platform, reporting on emerging risks and safety developments at leading AI labs.
key_arg: AI Safety China's Q4 2025 frontier AI monitoring platform reports stabilized overall risk levels across frontier models, though certain model families continue to present elevated risks requiring targeted mitigation.

name: Core Views on AI Safety
notes_head: Published by Anthropic in March 2023 as a company-wide statement of AI safety views and strategy. Frequently cited in AI governance discussions as representative of the 'responsible scaling' / 'pragmatic safety' frame, in contrast to pause-oriented positions. Often referenced alongside Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP).
key_arg: Anthropic's foundational public statement of its AI safety philosophy: frontier AI could be transformative and risky, safety research must scale alongside capabilities, empirical work on current models generalizes to future risks, and a mix of safety research and responsible scaling is necessary.

name: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
resource_author: Yuntao Bai, Saurav Kadavath, Sandipan Kundu, Amanda Askell, Jackson Kernion, Andy Jones, Anna Chen, Anna Goldie, Azalia Mirhoseini, Cameron McKinnon, et al. (Anthropic)
notes_head: Foundational Anthropic paper (December 2022) that introduced Constitutional AI, the training approach that underlies the Claude models' safety behavior. Widely cited in AI governance discussions around alignment techniques, scalable oversight, and alternatives to pure-human-feedback training.
key_arg: Introduces Constitutional AI (CAI): a method to train a harmless AI assistant using AI-generated feedback guided by a set of written principles ('constitution'), instead of relying primarily on human-labeled harmlessness feedback. Builds on RLHF but replaces human preference labels for harmfulness with AI self-critique and revision.

name: Training Language Models to Follow Instructions with Human Feedback
resource_author: Long Ouyang, Jeff Wu, Xu Jiang, Diogo Almeida, Carroll L. Wainwright, Pamela Mishkin, Chong Zhang, Sandhini Agarwal, Katarina Slama, Alex Ray, John Schulman, Jacob Hilton, Fraser Kelton, Luke Miller, Maddie Simens, Amanda Askell, Peter Welinder, Paul Christiano, Jan Leike, Ryan Lowe (OpenAI)
notes_head: OpenAI's foundational 2022 RLHF paper (the 'InstructGPT' paper), which underpins the alignment approach in ChatGPT and all subsequent commercial LLMs. Frequently cited in AI policy discussions about post-training interventions, alignment research, and the limits of current safety techniques.
key_arg: Shows that fine-tuning language models with reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) produces outputs humans prefer and rate as more truthful and less harmful than base models, even when the RLHF model is 100x smaller. Established RLHF as the dominant post-training alignment technique for commercial LLMs (used in ChatGPT, GPT-4, Claude, Gemini).

name: The Simple Macroeconomics of AI
notes_head: An economics paper analyzing the macroeconomic implications of AI adoption, including effects on productivity, labor markets, and economic growth.
key_arg: AI will have modest economic effects in the near term, with GDP growth of only 0.5-1.5% over 10 years.

name: Canaries in the Coal Mine? Six Facts about the Recent Employment Effects of Artificial Intelligence
resource_author: Erik Brynjolfsson, Danielle Li, Lindsey Raymond, Tom Sheehy
notes_head: Economics research paper presenting six key findings on the early employment effects of artificial intelligence, framing affected workers as ''canaries in the coal mine'' for broader labor market disruption.
key_arg: Analysis of recent AI employment effects shows generative AI adoption has disproportionately impacted entry-level workers aged 22-25 in AI-exposed occupations, with measurable wage and employment declines.

name: MIT study finds AI can already replace 11.7% of U.S. ...
notes_head: News article covering an MIT study finding that AI technology is already capable of replacing approximately 11.7% of tasks in the U.S. workforce.
key_arg: An MIT study estimates that AI can currently replace approximately 11.7% of the U.S. workforce (roughly 18 million workers), representing approximately $1.2 trillion in annual wage exposure.

name: Labor Demand in the Age of Generative AI : Early Evidence from the U.S. Job Posting Data
resource_author: David Autor, Simon Jäger, David Sands, Pascaul Restrepo
notes_head: Economics research paper analyzing how generative AI is affecting U.S. labor demand, using job posting data to identify early shifts in employer hiring patterns.
key_arg: U.S. job posting data reveals that generative AI tools like ChatGPT have led to significant declines in labor demand for certain occupational categories.

name: FACT SHEET: Biden-Harris Administration Executive Order Directs DHS to Lead the Responsible Development of Artificial Intelligence | Homeland Security
notes_head: Department of Homeland Security fact sheet detailing how the Biden-Harris Administration''s AI executive order directs DHS to lead responsible AI development, including safeguards for critical infrastructure and border security.
key_arg: The Biden-Harris Administration's executive order designates DHS as the lead agency for overseeing the responsible development and deployment of artificial intelligence across federal agencies.

name: Biden signs U.S.' first AI executive order to create safeguards
notes_head: News article covering President Biden''s signing of the first comprehensive U.S. executive order on artificial intelligence, which established safeguards around AI safety, security, and trustworthiness.
key_arg: President Biden signed the U.S.'s first comprehensive AI executive order, establishing binding safety, equity, privacy, and security standards for AI development and deployment.

name: Stochastic Parrots Paper
resource_author: Emily Bender, Timnit Gebru, et al.
notes_head: TEST DATA — Controversial paper that led to Gebru leaving Google; foundational for AI ethics discourse
key_arg: Large language models pose environmental, bias, and misinformation risks that are inadequately addressed

name: Machines of Loving Grace
notes_head: Essay by Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei (October 2024) exploring the potential positive impacts of advanced AI on science, health, economic development, and governance.
key_arg: AI development offers transformative benefits like disease curing and poverty reduction, but realizing this optimistic vision requires deliberate focus on safety and alignment alongside capability advances.

name: Transcript: Joint Hearing on “Artificial Intelligence and Its Potential to Fuel Economic Growth and Improve Governance” | TechPolicy.Press
notes_head: Transcript of a joint congressional hearing on artificial intelligence''s potential to fuel economic growth and improve governance, published by TechPolicy.Press.
key_arg: A congressional joint hearing examining artificial intelligence's potential to drive economic growth and improve governance through expert testimony on AI applications.

name: Attention Is All You Need
resource_author: Ashish Vaswani, Noam Shazeer, Niki Parmar, Jakob Uszkoreit, Llion Jones, Aidan N. Gomez, Łukasz Kaiser, Illia Polosukhin (Google)
notes_head: 2017 Google paper at NeurIPS that introduced the Transformer architecture — the foundation of GPT, BERT, Claude, Gemini, LLaMA, and essentially every frontier language model. Of the eight authors, Aidan N. Gomez later co-founded Cohere. The paper is routinely invoked in AI policy discussions to ground claims about the lineage and maturity of current AI systems.
key_arg: Introduces the Transformer, a neural network architecture based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions. Achieves state-of-the-art translation quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less training time. The architectural foundation of nearly all modern large language models.
